title: Microgramma lycopodioides
taxonomy: Microgramma lycopodioides is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, a family of ferns commonly known as the spleenworts. This species belongs to the genus Microgramma, a group of tropical and subtropical ferns. Microgramma lycopodioides is native to the Americas, particularly in the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. It is a terrestrial fern species, often found in shaded areas and humid environments. Microgramma lycopodioides is also known to be an epiphyte, growing on tree trunks and other substrates in its natural habitat.
uses: Microgramma lycopodioides is known to have various medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. It is reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential benefits.
